title: digitalis cariensis
intro: Digitalis cariensis, also known as the Carian foxglove or Carian digitalis, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, closely related to the common foxglove (Digitalis purpurea). The medicinal properties of Digitalis cariensis are attributed to its cardiac glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as heart failure, atrial fibrillation, and other cardiac disorders. The health benefits of Digitalis cariensis include improved heart function, increased cardiac output, and reduced symptoms of heart failure, making it a valuable herbal remedy for cardiovascular health. Digitalis cariensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
